# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)

EndSection

	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"

### Post by housam on 2008-06-22
Try to reset your resolution again.try the following :
System > Preferences > Screen Resolution and select the original.
__________________

### Post by PmDematagoda on 2008-06-22
ThomasWii:- A reason you might be getting these problems is because your VGA card is using the vesa driver which means it's not configured properly. Could you please post the specifications of your PC.

---

### Post by PmDematagoda on 2008-06-22
Install the unichrome drivers with:-
```
sudo apt-get install xserver-xorg-video-unichrome
```
reconfigure the X-Server with:-
```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```
and select the unichrome driver and any other options you may want, after that is done, reboot the PC and see if the case is improved.

---
